aids.title: Ethereum Layer Stack
aids.content:
**NCE** = Network, Consensus, Execution

"**N**ice **C**ats **E**at" - Remember Ethereum's three protocol layers from bottom to top!
aids.forRemembering: The three layers of Ethereum architecture

aids.title: STTR - Four Tries
aids.content:
**S**tate Trie - Account states
**T**ransaction Trie - Block transactions
**T**rie (Storage) - Contract storage
**R**eceipt Trie - Transaction receipts
aids.forRemembering: The four Merkle Patricia Tries in Ethereum

aids.title: The Global Computer Analogy
aids.content:
Think of Ethereum as a **worldwide computer**:
- **Network Layer** = Internet connecting all parts
- **Consensus Layer** = The operating system deciding what runs
- **Execution Layer** = The CPU executing programs
- **State** = RAM/Storage holding all data

Every node runs the same 'computer' ensuring consistency!

aids.title: Validator Magic Number
aids.content:
**32 ETH** = Minimum stake to become a validator

Remember: '32-bit' computers were standard, '32 ETH' is the standard stake!

aids.title: Block as a Container
aids.content:
Visualize an Ethereum block as a **shipping container**:
- Label (Header) = Block number, timestamp, parent hash
- Contents (Body) = Transactions packed inside
- Seal (Roots) = Merkle roots verifying contents
- Receipt = Gas used, logs, status

Containers chain together via parent hash links!

aids.title: Node Types Comparison
aids.content:
**Full Node** = Library with all current books
**Archive Node** = Library with all books ever written
**Light Node** = Library catalog only
**Validator Node** = Librarian who organizes new books
